title: Structured Observability, Health, Profiling, Capacity, and Incidents · Go Semantics Case: Http Metrics Cardinality
summary: Logs contain tokens and raw paths, metrics use user IDs, traces mark every 404 as a server error, readiness ignores database saturation, and a p50 benchmark drives release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo http metrics cardinality increment in low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure duration distributions, active requests, request and response bytes, status classes, cancellations, rejects, saturation, and dependencies with bounded attributes.
Emit one structured request completion record with request and trace IDs, method, route pattern, status, duration, bytes, trusted peer class, and redaction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
